rawText:
Chris Anderson: Demis, so good to have you here.

Demis Hassabis: It's fantastic to be here, thanks, Chris. Now, you told Time Magazine, "I want to understand the big questions, the really big ones that you normally go into philosophy or physics if you're interested in them. I thought building AI would be the fastest route to answer some of those questions." Why did you think that?

DH: Well, I guess when I was a kid, my favorite subject was physics, and I was interested in all the big questions, fundamental nature of reality, what is consciousness, you know, all the big ones. And usually you go into physics, if you're interested in that. But I read a lot of the great physicists, some of my all-time scientific heroes like Feynman and so on. And I realized, in the last, sort of 20, 30 years, we haven't made much progress in understanding some of these fundamental laws. So I thought, why not build the ultimate tool to help us, which is artificial intelligence. And at the same time, we could also maybe better understand ourselves and the brain better, by doing that too. So not only was it incredible tool, it was also useful for some of the big questions itself.

CA: Super interesting. So obviously AI can do so many things, but I think for this conversation, I'd love to focus in on this theme of what it might do to unlock the really big questions, the giant scientific breakthroughs, because it's been such a theme driving you and your company.

DH: So I mean, one of the big things AI can do, and I've always thought about, is we're getting, you know, even back 20, 30 years ago, the beginning of the internet era and computer era, the amount of data that was being produced and also scientific data, just too much for the human mind to comprehend in many cases. And I think one of the uses of AI is to find patterns and insights in huge amounts of data and then surface that to the human scientists to make sense of and make new hypotheses and conjectures. So it seems to me very compatible with the scientific method.

CA: Right. But game play has played a huge role in your own journey in figuring this thing out.

DH: I loved all kinds of games, yeah.

CA: And when you launched DeepMind, pretty quickly, you started having it tackle game play. Why?

DH: Well, look, I mean, games actually got me into AI in the first place because while we were doing things like, we used to go on training camps with the England team and so on. And actually back then, I guess it was in the mid '80s, we would use the very early chess computers, if you remember them, to train against, as well as playing against each other. And they were big lumps of plastic, you know, physical boards that you used to, some of you remember, used to actually press the squares down and there were LED lights, came on. And I remember actually, not just thinking about the chess, I was actually just fascinated by the fact that this lump of plastic, someone had programmed it to be smart and actually play chess to a really high standard. And I was just amazed by that. And that got me thinking about thinking. And how does the brain come up with these thought processes, these ideas, and then maybe how we could mimic that with computers. So yeah, it's been a whole theme for my whole life, really.

DH: Well, we started off with games at the beginning of DeepMind. This was back in 2010, so this is from about 10 years ago, it was our first big breakthrough. Because we started off with classic Atari games from the 1970s, the simplest kind of computer games there are out there. And one of the reasons we used games is they're very convenient to test out your ideas and your algorithms. They're really fast to test. And also, as your systems get more powerful, you can choose harder and harder games.

DH: It was a real revolution at the time. So this was in 2012, 2013 where we coined these terms "deep reinforcement learning." And the key thing about them is that those systems were learning directly from the pixels, the raw pixels on the screen, but they weren't being told anything else. So they were being told, maximize the score, here are the pixels on the screen, 30,000 pixels. The system has to make sense on its own from first principles what's going on, what it's controlling, how to get points.

overview.title: How AI Is Unlocking the Secrets of Nature and the Universe

sentences.original: Demis, so good to have you here.
sentences.translation: 데미스, 여기 와주셔서 정말 좋습니다.

vocabulary.example: It's so good to see you again.

expressions.phrase: so good to have you here
expressions.usage: 게스트를 환영하는 인사로 인터뷰나 토크쇼에서 자주 사용

patterns.pattern: So good to [verb] you [here/again/today]
patterns.explanation: 상대방을 환영할 때 사용하는 간결한 인사 패턴. "It's"가 생략된 구어체.
examples: So good to see you again!
So good to have you on the show.

drillQuestions.question: So good to _____ you here.
_____ good to see you again.

sentences.notes: "It's so good to have you here"에서 "It's"가 생략된 구어체 표현. TED 인터뷰에서 호스트가 게스트를 소개할 때 자주 쓰인다.

koreanAlignment.en: so good to have you here

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 환영 인사(greeting) — 간결한 호명으로 친밀한 대화 분위기를 조성

sentences.original: It's fantastic to be here, thanks, Chris.
sentences.translation: 여기 오게 되어서 정말 좋습니다, 감사합니다, 크리스.

vocabulary.example: The concert was fantastic.

expressions.usage: 초대에 대한 감사를 표현하는 응답

patterns.pattern: It's [adjective] to be [here/part of/involved in]
patterns.explanation: 가주어 it 구문으로 감정을 표현. 공식적 환영에 대한 응답으로 자주 사용.
examples: It's wonderful to be part of this event.
It's great to be back.

drillQuestions.question: It's fantastic _____ be here.
_____ great to be back.

sentences.notes: 가주어 it + 형용사 + to부정사 구문. "It is fantastic to be here"는 "To be here is fantastic"의 변형으로, 영어에서 주어가 긴 경우 it을 가주어로 사용하는 전형적 패턴.

sentences.original: I want to understand the big questions, the really big ones that you normally go into philosophy or physics if you're interested in them.
sentences.translation: 저는 큰 질문들을 이해하고 싶습니다. 관심이 있으면 보통 철학이나 물리학으로 가게 되는 정말 큰 질문들 말입니다.

grammarElements.text: that you normally go into philosophy or physics if you're interested in them
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that — ones를 수식)

vocabulary.example: She studied philosophy at university.
Physics explains the fundamental laws of nature.
I normally wake up at 7 AM.

expressions.usage: 특정 학문이나 직업 분야로 진출할 때
앞서 언급한 것을 강조하여 다시 지칭할 때. ones는 대명사.

patterns.pattern: the [adjective] ones that you normally [verb] if you're interested in [them]
patterns.explanation: 특정 대상을 관계대명사절로 정의하며, 조건절(if)로 맥락을 추가하는 패턴.
examples: The hard courses that you normally take if you want to challenge yourself.
The deep topics that you normally study if you want a PhD.

drillQuestions.question: I want to understand the big _____, the really big ones.
You normally go _____ philosophy if you're interested.

sentences.notes: "the really big ones"는 "the big questions"의 동격(apposition). ones는 앞의 questions를 대신하는 대명사. 관계대명사절 안에 조건절(if)이 내포된 복합 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: that you normally go into philosophy or physics

sentences.original: I thought building AI would be the fastest route to answer some of those questions.
sentences.translation: AI를 만드는 것이 그런 질문들에 답하는 가장 빠른 경로가 될 거라고 생각했습니다.

grammarElements.text: to answer some of those questions
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (route를 수식)

vocabulary.example: What is the fastest route to the airport?
Building a business takes time and effort.

expressions.usage: 목표 달성을 위한 최적의 방법을 표현
전부가 아닌 일부를 겸손하게 표현

patterns.pattern: I thought [gerund phrase] would be the [superlative] [noun] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 판단을 설명하는 패턴. 동명사 주어 + would be + 최상급 + to부정사.
examples: I thought studying abroad would be the best way to learn the language.
I thought starting early would be the safest approach to finish on time.

drillQuestions.question: I thought _____ AI would be the fastest route.
It would be the fastest route _____ answer those questions.

sentences.notes: "building AI"는 동명사구로 that절(생략)의 주어 역할. "would be"는 시제 일치(thought → would). route는 미국식 /ruːt/와 영국식 /raʊt/ 두 가지 발음이 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: to answer some of those questions

pronunciationNotes: route: /ruːt/ (미국) 또는 /raʊt/ (영국) — Hassabis는 영국식 발음 사용
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 개인적 동기 설명(personal motivation) — AI를 만든 이유를 질문에 대한 답으로 프레이밍

sentences.original: Well, I guess when I was a kid, my favorite subject was physics, and I was interested in all the big questions, fundamental nature of reality, what is consciousness, you know, all the big ones.
sentences.translation: 글쎄요, 제가 어렸을 때 가장 좋아하는 과목이 물리학이었고, 모든 큰 질문들에 관심이 있었어요. 현실의 근본적인 본질, 의식이란 무엇인가, 있잖아요, 그런 큰 것들 모두요.

grammarElements.text: fundamental nature of reality, what is consciousness
grammarElements.label: 동격 나열 (big questions의 예시)

vocabulary.example: This is a fundamental principle of science.
The nature of consciousness remains a mystery.
Virtual reality technology is advancing rapidly.

expressions.usage: 확신 없이 조심스럽게 말할 때. 구어체에서 매우 자주 사용
상대방의 공감을 유도하거나 말을 이어갈 때 쓰는 담화표지(filler)

patterns.pattern: When I was a kid, my favorite [noun] was [X], and I was interested in [Y]
patterns.explanation: 어린 시절의 관심사를 회상하며 현재의 일을 정당화하는 내러티브 패턴.
examples: When I was a kid, my favorite hobby was drawing, and I was interested in architecture.
When I was a kid, my favorite subject was biology, and I was interested in how things grow.

drillQuestions.question: I was interested _____ all the big questions.
My favorite _____ was physics.

sentences.notes: "I guess"와 "you know"는 구어체 담화표지(discourse markers)로, 원어민이 자연스러운 대화 흐름을 만들기 위해 자주 사용한다. 이 문장은 나열(enumeration) 구조로 "big questions"의 구체적 예시를 제시.

koreanAlignment.en: I was interested in all the big questions

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 개인적 일화(personal anecdote) — 어린 시절 이야기로 AI 연구의 원점을 청중에게 보여줌

sentences.original: But I read a lot of the great physicists, some of my all-time scientific heroes like Feynman and so on.
sentences.translation: 하지만 위대한 물리학자들의 책을 많이 읽었는데, 파인만 같은 역대 과학 영웅들이요.

grammarElements.text: a lot of the great physicists
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (환유: 물리학자 → 그들의 저서)

grammarElements.text: some of my all-time scientific heroes like Feynman
grammarElements.label: 동격구 (physicists를 부연)

vocabulary.example: Einstein was one of the greatest physicists in history.
This is one of my all-time favorite movies.
She is a hero to many young scientists.

expressions.usage: 추가 예시가 있지만 생략할 때
특정 분야에서 가장 존경하는 인물들을 표현

patterns.pattern: I read a lot of [people/authors], some of my [superlative] [noun] like [example]
patterns.explanation: 영향을 받은 인물들을 언급하며 동격구(apposition)로 구체적 예시를 추가하는 패턴.
examples: I listened to a lot of the classic composers, some of my all-time favorites like Bach.
I studied a lot of the great economists, some of my biggest influences like Keynes.

drillQuestions.question: I read a lot of the great _____.
Some of my all-time scientific _____ like Feynman.

sentences.notes: "read a lot of the great physicists"에서 "read the physicists"는 환유(metonymy) — 물리학자 자체를 읽은 것이 아니라 그들의 저서를 읽은 것. read의 과거형은 /red/로 발음이 달라짐에 주의.

koreanAlignment.en: But I read a lot of the great physicists
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 위대한 물리학자들의 책을 많이 읽었어요

koreanAlignment.en: some of my all-time scientific heroes

sentences.original: And I realized, in the last, sort of 20, 30 years, we haven't made much progress in understanding some of these fundamental laws.
sentences.translation: 그리고 깨달았어요, 지난 20, 30년 동안 이런 근본적인 법칙들을 이해하는 데 별로 진전이 없었다는 것을요.

grammarElements.text: in the last, sort of 20, 30 years
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사구 (삽입어 sort of 포함)

grammarElements.text: in understanding some of these fundamental laws
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (동명사 + 목적어)

vocabulary.example: I realized I had forgotten my keys.
We've made significant progress on the project.
The fundamental laws of physics govern the universe.

expressions.usage: 정확하지 않은 표현을 완화할 때. 구어체에서 매우 빈번
성과 부족을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: In the last [number] years, we haven't made much progress in [gerund]
patterns.explanation: 특정 기간 동안의 진전 부족을 지적하는 패턴. 현재완료로 현재까지의 누적을 표현.
examples: In the last decade, we haven't made much progress in reducing emissions.
In the last few years, we haven't made much progress in solving this problem.

drillQuestions.question: We haven't made much _____ in understanding these laws.
We haven't made much progress _____ understanding these laws.

sentences.notes: "sort of"는 영국 영어에서 특히 많이 쓰이는 담화표지로, "kind of"와 비슷한 역할. Hassabis가 "20, 30 years"처럼 대략적인 숫자를 제시할 때 완화 표현으로 사용.

koreanAlignment.en: in the last, sort of 20, 30 years
in understanding some of these fundamental laws

pronunciationNotes: progress (명사): /ˈprɑːɡres/ — "프라그레스", 강세는 첫 음절 (동사는 두 번째)

sentences.original: So I thought, why not build the ultimate tool to help us, which is artificial intelligence.
sentences.translation: 그래서 생각했죠, 우리를 도울 궁극의 도구를 만들지 않겠는가, 바로 인공지능을요.

vocabulary.example: This is the ultimate guide to learning English.
Artificial intelligence is transforming every industry.

expressions.usage: 제안이나 아이디어를 제시할 때. 수사적 질문으로 결심을 표현
가장 강력하거나 최종적인 수단을 표현

patterns.pattern: I thought, why not [verb] the [superlative/ultimate] [noun], which is [definition]
patterns.explanation: 자기 내면의 결심을 수사 의문문으로 표현하고, which로 정의를 추가하는 패턴.
examples: I thought, why not try the best approach, which is direct communication.
I thought, why not use the most powerful method, which is machine learning.

drillQuestions.question: Why _____ build the ultimate tool?
The ultimate tool to help us, _____ is artificial intelligence.

sentences.notes: "why not + 동사원형"은 제안이나 결심을 수사 의문문 형태로 표현하는 구어체 패턴. "which is artificial intelligence"는 관계대명사의 계속적 용법(non-restrictive)으로, 앞의 tool을 설명/정의한다.

koreanAlignment.en: why not build the ultimate tool

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — "why not"으로 AI 개발 결심의 자연스러움을 강조

sentences.original: And at the same time, we could also maybe better understand ourselves and the brain better, by doing that too.
sentences.translation: 그리고 동시에, 그렇게 함으로써 우리 자신과 뇌를 더 잘 이해할 수도 있을 거라고요.

vocabulary.example: You can learn and have fun at the same time.
The human brain is incredibly complex.

expressions.usage: 수단이나 방법을 전치사 by + 동명사로 표현
가능성을 조심스럽게 표현. 구어체에서 부사를 중첩하여 완화

patterns.pattern: At the same time, we could also [verb] ... by [gerund]
patterns.explanation: 주요 목적 외에 부수적 이점을 제시하는 패턴. "동시에 ~도 가능하다"는 구조.
examples: At the same time, we could also reduce costs by automating the process.
At the same time, we could also learn new skills by working on this project.

drillQuestions.question: At the same time, we could also better understand ourselves _____ doing that.
_____ the same time, we could also learn about the brain.

sentences.notes: "better"가 두 번 나타나는 것은 즉흥 발언(impromptu speech)의 특징. 첫 번째 better는 동사 understand를 수식하고, 두 번째는 실수로 반복된 것. 구어체의 자연스러운 불완전함이 드러나는 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: we could also maybe better understand
koreanAlignment.ko: 더 잘 이해할 수도 있을 것이다

sentences.original: So not only was it incredible tool, it was also useful for some of the big questions itself.
sentences.translation: 그래서 놀라운 도구일 뿐만 아니라, 그 자체로도 큰 질문들에 유용했습니다.

grammarElements.label: 도치 구문 (not only 뒤 주어-동사 도치)

grammarElements.text: for some of the big questions
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상)

vocabulary.example: The view from the mountaintop was incredible.
This information is very useful for our research.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 이점을 모두 강조할 때
재귀대명사의 강조 용법으로, 주어를 다시 강조

patterns.pattern: Not only was it [adjective noun], it was also [adjective] for [noun]
patterns.explanation: not only 도치 구문. not only가 문두에 오면 주어-동사가 도치된다. 두 가지 이점을 강조.
examples: Not only was it a great opportunity, it was also a turning point for my career.
Not only was it a useful technique, it was also easy to implement.

drillQuestions.question: Not only _____ it an incredible tool, it was also useful.
It was useful for some of the big questions _____.

sentences.notes: not only가 문두에 오면 도치(inversion)가 발생: "it was" → "was it". 구어체에서 "but"이 종종 생략된다. "itself"는 AI(it)를 가리키며, "AI가 그 자체로 큰 질문에 대한 탐구"라는 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: not only was it incredible tool
for some of the big questions

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 도치(inversion) + 상관접속사 — 두 가지 가치(도구 + 탐구 대상)를 동시에 강조하며 결론

sentences.original: So obviously AI can do so many things, but I think for this conversation, I'd love to focus in on this theme of what it might do to unlock the really big questions, the giant scientific breakthroughs, because it's been such a theme driving you and your company.
sentences.translation: 물론 AI는 정말 많은 것을 할 수 있지만, 이 대화에서는 정말 큰 질문들, 거대한 과학적 돌파구를 여는 데 AI가 무엇을 할 수 있을지에 초점을 맞추고 싶습니다. 그것이 당신과 당신의 회사를 이끌어온 주제이니까요.

grammarElements.text: AI can do so many things
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (but으로 전환)

grammarElements.text: I'd love to focus in on
grammarElements.label: 동사 (would love to + 구동사)

grammarElements.text: this theme of what it might do to unlock
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (명사 + of + 간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: the really big questions, the giant scientific breakthroughs
grammarElements.label: 동격 (questions와 breakthroughs 병렬)

grammarElements.text: because it's been such a theme driving you and your company
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (because)

vocabulary.example: This discovery could unlock new possibilities.
The vaccine was a major scientific breakthrough.
The main theme of the conference was sustainability.

expressions.phrase: I'd love to focus in on
expressions.usage: "I'd love to"는 "I want to"보다 정중한 표현. focus in on은 focus on의 강화형.
such + 명사로 강조, 현재분사(driving)가 theme을 수식

patterns.pattern: I'd love to focus in on [topic], because it's been such a [noun] [driving/shaping] [object]
patterns.explanation: 대화의 방향을 설정하면서 이유를 제시하는 진행자용 패턴.
examples: I'd love to focus in on education, because it's been such a priority driving policy.
I'd love to focus in on sustainability, because it's been such a goal shaping the industry.

drillQuestions.question: I'd love to focus _____ on this theme.
It's been such a theme _____ you and your company.

sentences.notes: "focus in on"은 "focus on"보다 더 좁은 범위에 집중한다는 뉘앙스. "such a theme"에서 such는 강조 기능. 이 문장은 인터뷰어가 대화의 방향을 전환하는 전형적인 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: obviously AI can do so many things
koreanAlignment.ko: 물론 AI는 정말 많은 것을 할 수 있지만

koreanAlignment.en: I'd love to focus in on this theme
of what it might do to unlock
the really big questions, the giant scientific breakthroughs
koreanAlignment.ko: 정말 큰 질문들, 거대한 과학적 돌파구를 여는 데

koreanAlignment.en: because it's been such a theme driving you and your company

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 프레이밍(framing) — 인터뷰어가 대화의 범위를 좁혀 핵심 주제를 설정

sentences.original: One of the big things AI can do, and I've always thought about, is we're getting, you know, even back 20, 30 years ago, the beginning of the internet era and computer era, the amount of data that was being produced and also scientific data, just too much for the human mind to comprehend in many cases.
sentences.translation: AI가 할 수 있는 큰 일 중 하나는, 그리고 제가 항상 생각해왔던 것은, 이미 20, 30년 전 인터넷 시대와 컴퓨터 시대가 시작될 때부터 생산되는 데이터의 양이, 과학 데이터도 마찬가지로, 많은 경우 인간의 두뇌가 이해하기에는 너무 많다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: One of the big things AI can do
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: the amount of data that was being produced
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: just too much for the human mind to comprehend
grammarElements.label: 보어 (too ... to 구문)

vocabulary.example: The sheer scale of the universe is hard to comprehend.
We are living in the digital era.
The amount of information online is staggering.

expressions.phrase: too much for the human mind to comprehend
expressions.usage: too ... to 구문으로 능력의 한계를 표현
even으로 시간적 거리를 강조, 그때부터 이미 그랬다는 뉘앙스

patterns.pattern: The amount of [noun] ... too much for [someone] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: too ... to 구문으로 데이터나 정보의 과부하를 표현하는 패턴.
examples: The amount of research being published is too much for any one person to read.
The amount of code in the project is too much for a single developer to maintain.

drillQuestions.question: The amount of data was too much for the human mind to _____.
The _____ of data being produced was enormous.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 구어체 특유의 복잡한 구조를 가진다. 화자가 여러 생각을 동시에 풀어내면서 삽입절("and I've always thought about")과 부가 설명("even back 20, 30 years ago")이 중첩된다. too ... for ... to 구문이 핵심.

koreanAlignment.en: One of the big things AI can do
koreanAlignment.ko: AI가 할 수 있는 큰 일 중 하나는

koreanAlignment.en: the amount of data that was being produced
just too much for the human mind to comprehend

sentences.original: And I think one of the uses of AI is to find patterns and insights in huge amounts of data and then surface that to the human scientists to make sense of and make new hypotheses and conjectures.
sentences.translation: 그리고 AI의 용도 중 하나는 방대한 양의 데이터에서 패턴과 통찰을 찾아내고, 그것을 인간 과학자들에게 표면화하여 이해하고 새로운 가설과 추론을 세울 수 있게 하는 것이라고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: one of the uses of AI
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: to find patterns and insights in huge amounts of data
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to부정사 — 첫 번째 용도)

grammarElements.text: and then surface that to the human scientists
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to부정사 — 두 번째 용도)

grammarElements.text: to make sense of and make new hypotheses and conjectures
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적)

vocabulary.example: The study provides valuable insights into consumer behavior.
New evidence surfaced during the investigation.
The scientist tested her hypothesis in the lab.
At this point, it's pure conjecture.

expressions.usage: 복잡한 것을 이해 가능한 것으로 만들 때
데이터 분석의 목적을 표현

patterns.pattern: One of the uses of [X] is to [verb A] and then [verb B] to [verb C]
patterns.explanation: 기술의 활용 방법을 순차적으로 나열하는 패턴. 발견 → 전달 → 활용의 흐름.
examples: One of the uses of data analytics is to identify trends and then report them to managers to make decisions.
One of the uses of satellite imagery is to detect changes and then alert scientists to investigate further.

drillQuestions.question: One of the uses of AI is to find _____ in huge amounts of data.
Surface that to the scientists to make _____ of.

sentences.notes: "surface"가 동사로 사용된 점이 특이하다. 보통 명사(표면)로 쓰이지만, 여기서는 "표면 위로 올리다, 드러내다"는 뜻의 동사. "hypotheses"는 hypothesis의 복수형(/haɪˈpɑːθəsiːz/). conjecture는 hypothesis보다 증거가 덜 확실한 추론.

koreanAlignment.en: one of the uses of AI is
and then surface that to the human scientists
and make new hypotheses and conjectures
koreanAlignment.ko: 새로운 가설과 추론을 세울 수 있게

pronunciationNotes: hypotheses: /haɪˈpɑːθəsiːz/ — "하이파서시즈", hypothesis의 복수형. 강세는 두 번째 음절
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 순차적 논증(sequential argument) — AI의 역할을 발견→전달→활용의 3단계로 설명

sentences.original: So it seems to me very compatible with the scientific method.
sentences.translation: 그래서 저에게는 과학적 방법론과 매우 양립 가능한 것으로 보입니다.

vocabulary.example: This software is compatible with most devices.
The scientific method involves testing hypotheses through experiments.

expressions.usage: 주관적 의견을 조심스럽게 제시할 때
두 개념이나 시스템이 함께 작동할 수 있을 때

patterns.pattern: It seems to me very [adjective] with [noun]
patterns.explanation: 개인적 판단을 완화하여 표현하는 패턴. "seems to me"로 주관성을 명시.
examples: It seems to me very consistent with the evidence.
It seems to me very aligned with our goals.

drillQuestions.question: It seems _____ me very compatible with the scientific method.
AI is very compatible _____ the scientific method.

sentences.notes: "it seems to me"는 "I think"보다 더 완화된 의견 표현. "compatible with the scientific method"는 AI가 과학과 대립하는 것이 아니라 보완적이라는 Hassabis의 핵심 주장.

sentences.original: But game play has played a huge role in your own journey in figuring this thing out.
sentences.translation: 하지만 게임 플레이가 이것을 알아내는 당신의 여정에서 큰 역할을 했죠.

vocabulary.example: Education plays a huge role in economic development.
I need to figure out how this works.
The journey from idea to product was long.

expressions.phrase: has played a huge role in
expressions.usage: 어떤 요소가 중요한 기여를 했음을 표현
문제를 해결하거나 이해하는 과정을 구어적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has played a huge role in [possessive] journey in [gerund]
patterns.explanation: 현재완료로 과거부터 현재까지의 영향을 표현. 역할/기여를 강조하는 패턴.
examples: Mentorship has played a huge role in my journey in becoming a leader.
Technology has played a huge role in our journey in improving healthcare.

drillQuestions.question: Game play has played a huge _____ in your journey.
A huge role in _____ this thing out.

sentences.notes: "play a role"의 "play"와 "game play"의 "play"가 같은 문장에 등장하여 일종의 언어 유희(word play)를 만든다. 현재완료(has played)는 과거부터 현재까지의 지속적 영향을 나타낸다.

sentences.original: And when you launched DeepMind, pretty quickly, you started having it tackle game play. Why?
sentences.translation: 그리고 DeepMind를 시작했을 때, 꽤 빠르게 게임 플레이에 도전하게 했잖아요. 왜요?

vocabulary.example: They launched a new product last month.
We need to tackle this problem head-on.
He learned to code pretty quickly.

expressions.usage: have + 목적어 + 동사원형(사역동사 구문)을 start로 감싼 구조

patterns.pattern: When you [past verb], pretty quickly, you started [gerund]
patterns.explanation: 시작 후 빠르게 진행된 행동을 묘사하는 인터뷰 질문 패턴.
examples: When you joined the company, pretty quickly, you started changing things.
When you moved there, pretty quickly, you started making friends.

drillQuestions.question: When you _____ DeepMind, you started tackling games.
You started having it _____ game play.

sentences.notes: "having it tackle"은 사역동사 have(~하게 하다) 구문. "it"은 DeepMind/AI를 가리킨다. 마지막의 "Why?"는 단독 의문문으로, 인터뷰에서 핵심 질문을 던지는 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: you started having it tackle game play

sentences.original: Games actually got me into AI in the first place because while we were doing things like, we used to go on training camps with the England team and so on.
sentences.translation: 사실 게임이 처음에 저를 AI에 빠지게 했어요. 잉글랜드 팀과 함께 훈련 캠프에 가곤 했거든요.

grammarElements.text: we used to go on training camps
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (used to — 과거 습관)

vocabulary.example: How did you get into programming?
Why did you apply in the first place?
We used to play in the park every weekend.
The team went to a training camp before the season.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것에 관심을 갖게 된 계기를 설명할 때
원래의 동기나 시작점을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] actually got me into [field/hobby] in the first place because ...
patterns.explanation: 관심사의 원래 계기를 설명하는 패턴. "actually"로 의외성을 강조.
examples: A book actually got me into philosophy in the first place.
My father actually got me into cooking in the first place.

drillQuestions.question: Games actually got me _____ AI in the first place.
We _____ to go on training camps with the England team.

sentences.notes: Hassabis는 어린 시절 체스 선수로 영국 대표팀에 발탁된 신동이었다. "used to"는 과거의 반복적 습관을 나타내며 현재는 더 이상 하지 않음을 함축.

koreanAlignment.en: Games actually got me into AI
koreanAlignment.ko: 사실 게임이 저를 AI에 빠지게 했어요

koreanAlignment.en: we used to go on training camps

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 개인적 일화(personal anecdote) — 체스 선수 경력과 AI의 연결고리를 청중에게 보여줌

sentences.original: And I remember actually, not just thinking about the chess, I was actually just fascinated by the fact that this lump of plastic, someone had programmed it to be smart and actually play chess to a really high standard.
sentences.translation: 그리고 기억나는 건, 체스 자체를 생각한 게 아니라, 이 플라스틱 덩어리에 누군가가 프로그래밍해서 정말 높은 수준으로 체스를 둘 수 있게 만들었다는 사실에 매료되었다는 거예요.

grammarElements.text: not just thinking about the chess
grammarElements.label: 부정 부사구 (not just — 단지 ~뿐만 아니라)

grammarElements.text: someone had programmed it to be smart
grammarElements.label: that절 (과거완료)

vocabulary.example: I was fascinated by the complexity of the human brain.
He placed a lump of clay on the table.
The product meets high safety standards.

expressions.usage: 놀라운 사실이 관심을 끈 이유를 설명할 때
수행 능력의 수준을 표현할 때
기계를 일부러 단순한 물체로 묘사하여 그 안의 지능을 대조적으로 강조

patterns.pattern: I was fascinated by the fact that [simple thing] ... [could do something impressive]
patterns.explanation: 단순한 것이 놀라운 능력을 가진다는 대비를 통해 경이감을 표현하는 패턴.
examples: I was fascinated by the fact that a tiny chip could process millions of calculations.
I was fascinated by the fact that a simple algorithm could recognize faces.

drillQuestions.question: I was fascinated _____ the fact that a machine could play chess.
Someone had programmed it _____ be smart.

sentences.notes: "lump of plastic"은 의도적으로 비하적인 표현으로, 외형의 단순함과 내부의 지능을 극적으로 대비시킨다. "actually"가 세 번 반복되는 것은 구어체의 자연스러운 강조 습관.

koreanAlignment.en: not just thinking about the chess
I was fascinated by the fact that
someone had programmed it to be smart
and actually play chess to a really high standard
koreanAlignment.ko: 정말 높은 수준으로 체스를 둘 수 있게

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비(contrast) — 물리적 단순함(플라스틱 덩어리)과 지적 복잡성(체스 고수)의 극적 대조

sentences.original: And that got me thinking about thinking.
sentences.translation: 그리고 그것이 "생각에 대해 생각하게" 만들었어요.

vocabulary.example: That question really got me thinking.

expressions.usage: 메타인지(metacognition)를 구어체로 표현. "thinking"의 반복이 인상적인 효과를 만듦

patterns.pattern: That got me thinking about [noun/gerund]
patterns.explanation: 특정 경험이 새로운 사고를 촉발했음을 표현하는 패턴. "got + 목적어 + -ing" 사역 구문.
examples: That book got me thinking about the meaning of life.
The documentary got me thinking about climate change.

drillQuestions.question: That got me _____ about thinking.
That got _____ thinking about AI.

sentences.notes: "thinking about thinking"은 같은 단어의 의도적 반복으로, 메타인지(metacognition) 개념을 간결하고 기억에 남게 표현한다. 이 한 문장이 Hassabis의 AI 연구 동기 전체를 함축.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복법(epizeuxis/polyptoton) — "thinking about thinking"으로 메타인지를 간결하고 인상적으로 표현

sentences.original: We started off with games at the beginning of DeepMind. This was back in 2010, so this is from about 10 years ago, it was our first big breakthrough.
sentences.translation: DeepMind 초기에 게임으로 시작했습니다. 이건 2010년의 일이니까 약 10년 전이고, 우리의 첫 번째 큰 돌파구였습니다.

grammarElements.text: it was our first big breakthrough
grammarElements.label: 주절

vocabulary.example: We started off with a simple plan.
The discovery was a major breakthrough in cancer research.

expressions.usage: back을 넣어 과거와의 시간적 거리감을 강조
프로젝트나 여정의 시작점을 설명할 때

patterns.pattern: We started off with [X] at the beginning of [Y]. It was our first big [noun].
patterns.explanation: 프로젝트의 시작과 첫 번째 성과를 설명하는 내러티브 패턴.
examples: We started off with small experiments at the beginning of the research. It was our first big discovery.
We started off with basic features at the beginning of the product. It was our first big release.

drillQuestions.question: We started _____ with games at the beginning of DeepMind.
It was our first big _____.

sentences.notes: "start off with"는 "begin with"보다 구어적. "This was back in 2010, so this is from about 10 years ago"에서 "this was"(과거)와 "this is"(현재)의 시제 전환은 강연 현장에서의 시간 인식을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: it was our first big breakthrough

sentences.original: And one of the reasons we used games is they're very convenient to test out your ideas and your algorithms.
sentences.translation: 그리고 게임을 사용한 이유 중 하나는 아이디어와 알고리즘을 시험하기에 매우 편리하다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: to test out your ideas and your algorithms
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (목적/형용사 수식)

vocabulary.example: Is this a convenient time to talk?
The algorithm sorts data efficiently.
Let's test out this new approach.

expressions.usage: 여러 이유 중 하나를 설명할 때
"test"보다 더 구어적이고 실험적인 뉘앙스

patterns.pattern: One of the reasons [clause] is [they're/it's] very [adjective] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 이유를 설명하는 패턴. 관계부사 why나 관계대명사가 생략된 구어체.
examples: One of the reasons we chose Python is it's very easy to learn.
One of the reasons I moved here is it's very close to work.

drillQuestions.question: One of the _____ we used games is convenience.
They're very convenient to test _____ your ideas.

sentences.notes: "one of the reasons we used games is"에서 관계부사 why가 생략되었다. "algorithm"은 9세기 페르시아 수학자 알 콰리즈미(al-Khwarizmi)의 이름에서 유래. 발음에 주의.

koreanAlignment.en: one of the reasons we used games
to test out your ideas and your algorithms

pronunciationNotes: algorithm: /ˈælɡərɪðəm/ — "앨거리듬", th는 /ð/ 발음, 강세는 첫 음절

sentences.original: It was a real revolution at the time.
sentences.translation: 당시로서는 진정한 혁명이었습니다.

vocabulary.example: The industrial revolution changed the world.
At the time, no one believed it was possible.

expressions.usage: 특정 사건의 혁신적 성격을 강조할 때
과거의 특정 시점을 지칭하며 현재와의 차이를 암시

patterns.pattern: It was a real [noun] at the time.
patterns.explanation: 과거 사건의 중요성을 회고적으로 평가하는 간결한 패턴.
examples: It was a real game-changer at the time.
It was a real milestone at the time.

drillQuestions.question: It was a real revolution _____ the time.

sentences.notes: 짧고 간결한 평가 문장. "at the time"은 "그때는 혁명적이었다(지금은 당연하지만)"라는 함축을 담고 있어, 기술의 빠른 발전을 암시한다.

sentences.original: The key thing about them is that those systems were learning directly from the pixels, the raw pixels on the screen, but they weren't being told anything else.
sentences.translation: 그것들에 대한 핵심은 그 시스템들이 화면의 픽셀, 가공되지 않은 원시 픽셀로부터 직접 학습하고 있었는데, 그 외에는 아무것도 알려주지 않았다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: those systems were learning directly from the pixels
grammarElements.label: that절 (과거진행)

grammarElements.text: the raw pixels on the screen
grammarElements.label: 동격 (pixels를 부연)

grammarElements.text: they weren't being told anything else
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (수동태 과거진행 부정)

vocabulary.example: The image is made up of millions of pixels.
The raw data needs to be processed before analysis.
The sensor reads data directly from the environment.

expressions.usage: 가장 중요한 포인트를 강조할 때
과거진행 수동태로 진행 중이던 상태를 표현

patterns.pattern: The key thing about [X] is that [they] were [verb]-ing from [source], but they weren't being [past participle] anything else
patterns.explanation: 기술의 핵심 특징을 설명하되, 제한 조건을 대비시키는 패턴.
examples: The key thing about the model is that it was learning from raw text, but it wasn't being given any labels.
The key thing about the robot is that it was navigating from sensor data, but it wasn't being guided by humans.

drillQuestions.question: The key thing _____ them is that they learned from pixels.
They were learning directly _____ the raw pixels.

sentences.notes: "weren't being told"은 과거진행 수동태(were + being + past participle)의 부정형으로, 가장 복잡한 시제 형태 중 하나. "raw pixels"은 전처리 없이 화면에서 바로 가져온 원시 데이터를 의미하며, 이것이 당시 혁신의 핵심이었다.

koreanAlignment.en: The key thing about them is that
those systems were learning directly from the pixels
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 시스템들이 픽셀로부터 직접 학습하고 있었다

koreanAlignment.en: the raw pixels on the screen
but they weren't being told anything else
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 그 외에는 아무것도 알려주지 않았다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비(contrast) — 학습 입력(raw pixels만)과 학습 제한(다른 정보 없음)을 대조하여 혁신성 강조

sentences.original: So they were being told, maximize the score, here are the pixels on the screen, 30,000 pixels. The system has to make sense on its own from first principles what's going on, what it's controlling, how to get points.
sentences.translation: 점수를 최대화하라, 여기 화면의 픽셀이 있다, 3만 개의 픽셀. 이것만 알려줬습니다. 시스템은 기본 원리에서 스스로 무슨 일이 벌어지고 있는지, 무엇을 조종하고 있는지, 어떻게 점수를 얻는지를 파악해야 했습니다.

grammarElements.text: here are the pixels on the screen
grammarElements.label: 제시 구문 (here are)

grammarElements.text: The system has to make sense
grammarElements.label: 주절 (의무)

grammarElements.text: what's going on, what it's controlling, how to get points
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 3개 병렬

vocabulary.example: We need to maximize our efficiency.
He approaches every problem from first principles.
It takes time to make sense of all this data.
The AI learned to solve the puzzle on its own.

expressions.usage: 기존 지식이나 편법 없이 근본부터 시작하는 접근법

expressions.phrase: make sense of ... on its own
expressions.usage: 외부 도움 없이 독립적으로 이해하는 것을 표현

patterns.pattern: The system has to [verb] on its own from first principles [what/how/why ...]
patterns.explanation: 시스템이 독립적으로 기본 원리에서 스스로 학습하는 과정을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: The robot has to navigate on its own from first principles where the obstacles are.
The model has to figure out on its own from first principles how language works.

drillQuestions.question: The system has to make sense on _____ own.
It learns from _____ principles.

sentences.notes: "from first principles"는 물리학에서 유래한 표현으로, Elon Musk도 자주 사용하는 개념. 기존 관례나 지식에 의존하지 않고 가장 기본적인 진실에서 시작하여 추론한다는 의미. 이것이 deep reinforcement learning의 핵심 원리.

koreanAlignment.en: they were being told, maximize the score
here are the pixels on the screen, 30,000 pixels
The system has to make sense on its own

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 열거(enumeration) — 세 개의 간접의문문을 병렬하여 시스템이 해결해야 할 과제의 범위를 강조
